features.summary: UnitedHealth Group reported strong Q4 2021 results with over $30 billion in revenue growth, about $10 billion above initial outlook. The company added 500,000 new patients to value-based care arrangements and expects up to 800,000 new Medicare Advantage members in 2022. OptumHealth revenue per consumer grew over 30%, OptumInsight earnings grew 25% with 28% operating margins, and OptumRx earnings grew 6%. Cash flow from operations reached $22.3 billion, exceeding initial outlook by $2 billion.
features.management_outlook: Management expressed heightened confidence in executing their five key growth areas: value-based care delivery, health benefits, health technology partnerships, health financial services, and pharmacy services. They maintained 2022 EPS guidance of $21.10-$21.60 and expect cash flow to approach $24 billion. The company anticipates continued strong Medicare Advantage growth with about 75% of new members in individual and group MA plans, while expecting modest Medicaid attrition as states resume eligibility redeterminations.
features.risks: COVID-19 continues to impact operations with higher attrition, unplanned absences, and care pattern disruptions including 10% decline in primary care visits and higher specialist visit declines during Omicron surge. Labor market pressures affect clinical talent, technology, and customer service roles. The company faces ongoing competitive pressures in Medicare Advantage markets and uncertainty around the cost impact of new federal at-home COVID testing requirements.
features.key_drivers: Value-based care expansion remains the primary growth driver with significant investments in clinical training, technology systems, and network coordination to support new patient transitions. Medicare Advantage market share gains are supported by OptumCare integration, quality improvements (99% of members in 4+ star plans), and innovative products. Specialty pharmacy automation and upcoming generic/biosimilar opportunities in 2022-2023 are expected to drive OptumRx growth and margin expansion.
